wins.title: All Quiet on the Western Front
You Can't Take It with You
Rebecca
wins.plotSummary: A shy young woman marries the wealthy, brooding widower Maxim de Winter and moves into his grand estate, Manderley, only to find herself constantly overshadowed by the memory of his glamorous first wife, Rebecca, who died under mysterious circumstances. The sinister housekeeper Mrs. Danvers, still fiercely devoted to Rebecca, works to convince the new bride she can never measure up, until dark secrets about Rebecca's death and true character finally surface. Alfred Hitchcock's gothic thriller follows the unnamed heroine's growing paranoia as Manderley's buried truths threaten to destroy her marriage and her sanity.

wins.title: How Green Was My Valley
wins.plotSummary: Told through the memories of youngest son Huw, the film follows the Morgan family, Welsh coal miners in a tight-knit valley community, as industrial decline, labor strikes, and tragedy in the mines gradually erode the close bonds and traditions that once defined their lives. Huw's father and brothers clash over unionization even as the family weathers personal loss, romance, and the slow disappearance of the valley's beauty to mining slag. John Ford's elegiac drama mourns a vanishing way of life through one boy's nostalgic, bittersweet coming of age.

wins.title: Mrs. Miniver
wins.plotSummary: Kay Miniver, an unassuming English housewife in a comfortable village outside London, watches her ordinary domestic life transform as World War II arrives at her doorstep, from her son's romance with a local aristocrat's granddaughter to her husband's participation in the Dunkirk evacuation. As German bombing raids bring the war directly home, the Minivers and their community must find quiet courage and resilience amid loss and disruption. William Wyler's wartime drama turns an English village's steadfastness under siege into a rallying symbol of civilian endurance.

wins.title: Casablanca
wins.plotSummary: Rick Blaine, a cynical American expatriate running a nightclub in Vichy-controlled Casablanca, is forced to confront his buried feelings when his former lover Ilsa arrives seeking exit visas for herself and her husband, a celebrated Czech resistance leader fleeing the Nazis. As Rick navigates corrupt officials, black-market dealings, and his own wounded pride, he must decide whether to help Ilsa's husband escape or keep her for himself. Michael Curtiz's wartime romance builds to Rick's legendary act of self-sacrifice at a fog-shrouded airport, choosing the greater cause over his own heartbreak.

wins.title: Going My Way
wins.plotSummary: Father Chuck O'Malley, a young, unconventional priest, arrives to take over a struggling New York parish from its elderly, traditionalist pastor Father Fitzgibbon, immediately clashing with the older man's rigid ways even as he wins over the neighborhood's troubled youth through music and charm. As O'Malley works to save the parish from foreclosure and mentor a group of wayward boys into a choir, he and Fitzgibbon slowly build a genuine friendship across their generational divide. Leo McCarey's warm-hearted comedy-drama, built around Bing Crosby's crooning, finds gentle humor and faith in the meeting of old and new ways.

wins.title: The Lost Weekend
wins.plotSummary: Don Birnam, a chronic alcoholic writer, tricks his brother and girlfriend into leaving him alone for a weekend so he can indulge his addiction uninterrupted, spiraling almost immediately into a desperate hunt for hidden bottles and pawnshop cash for liquor. As the days blur into a hallucinatory bender marked by shame, lies, and a terrifying stint in an alcoholic ward, Don's writing ambitions and self-respect collapse entirely under the weight of his compulsion. Billy Wilder's unflinching drama follows Don's rock-bottom weekend as one of the first honest Hollywood portrayals of addiction.

wins.title: The Best Years of Our Lives
wins.plotSummary: Three American servicemen, an infantry sergeant, an Army Air Forces captain, and a sailor who lost both hands in the war, return home to the same Midwestern town after World War II and struggle to readjust to civilian life, changed families, and a society that doesn't fully understand what they've been through. Each man confronts a different form of dislocation, from marital strain and class friction to the sailor's fear that his disability will drive away the woman who loves him. William Wyler's landmark drama treats veterans' postwar trauma and reintegration with unusual honesty for its era.

wins.title: Gentleman's Agreement
wins.plotSummary: Widowed journalist Phil Green, assigned to write a magazine exposé on antisemitism in New York and its wealthy suburbs, decides the only way to truly understand the subject is to pose as Jewish himself, and is stunned by the casual bigotry he encounters from colleagues, neighbors, and even his own fiancée. As his fake identity exposes uncomfortable truths about supposedly liberal, tolerant society, Phil's relationship with his fiancée Kathy is tested by her own unexamined prejudices. Elia Kazan's drama confronts genteel, "polite" antisemitism as a form of complicity every bit as damaging as open hatred.

wins.title: Hamlet
wins.plotSummary: Laurence Olivier's stripped-down adaptation of Shakespeare's tragedy follows Prince Hamlet of Denmark as he is visited by his father's ghost, who reveals he was murdered by Hamlet's uncle Claudius, now married to Hamlet's mother and sitting on the throne. Consumed by grief, suspicion, and a paralyzing inability to act, Hamlet feigns madness while plotting to confirm Claudius's guilt and avenge his father, dragging nearly everyone around him into the resulting catastrophe. Olivier's film, heavily trimmed from the four-hour play, became the first British film and first Shakespeare adaptation to win the Academy Award for Best Picture.

wins.title: All the King's Men
wins.plotSummary: Idealistic small-town lawyer Willie Stark runs for office promising to fight the corruption of Southern political machines, and his earnest campaign draws the attention of reporter Jack Burden, who writes a glowing profile that helps launch Stark's career. As Stark rises to the governorship, he abandons his early idealism for the same ruthless, manipulative tactics he once opposed, using threats, bribery, and blackmail to consolidate absolute power. Robert Rossen's drama, loosely based on Huey Long, follows Stark's corruption arc alongside Burden's own compromised complicity in the machine he helped build.

wins.title: All About Eve
wins.plotSummary: Ambitious young fan Eve Harrington maneuvers her way into the life of aging, insecure Broadway star Margo Channing, initially posing as a devoted admirer before methodically undermining Margo's career and relationships from the inside. As Eve's calculated rise threatens to eclipse Margo entirely, the people around them, from a cynical theater critic to Margo's own friends, begin to see through Eve's manipulations too late to stop her. Joseph L. Mankiewicz's acid-tongued drama frames Eve's ascent as a cautionary tale about ambition, aging, and the theater's ruthless appetite for the next rising star.

wins.title: An American in Paris
wins.plotSummary: Jerry Mulligan, an American ex-GI turned struggling painter in Paris, falls for the charming young Lise, unaware she is secretly engaged to the man who saved her family during the war. Jerry's own patron and would-be lover, wealthy heiress Milo, complicates matters further as the two couples' entanglements play out through the city's cafes and streets. Vincente Minnelli's musical builds to an extended, dialogue-free ballet sequence set entirely to Gershwin's music, resolving the romantic triangle through dance rather than words.

wins.title: The Greatest Show on Earth
wins.plotSummary: Brad Braden, general manager of the world's largest traveling circus, fights to keep the show on the road for a full season despite the board's demand for a shorter, safer run, while juggling a tangle of romantic rivalries among his star performers. New headline aerialist "The Great Sebastian" upends the pecking order and reignites old jealousies, and a mysterious clown named Buttons, who never removes his makeup, hides a secret of his own. Cecil B. DeMille's sprawling epic builds toward a catastrophic train wreck that forces the whole circus family to pull together to save the show.

wins.title: From Here to Eternity
wins.plotSummary: In the months before Pearl Harbor, Private Robert E. Lee Prewitt, a stubborn former boxer, is harassed by his commanding officer for refusing to fight on the base boxing team, while First Sergeant Milt Warden carries on a dangerous affair with the captain's neglected wife. Prewitt's own romance with a hostess at a local social club deepens even as army politics and violence close in around him and his loyal friend Maggio. Fred Zinnemann's drama follows the soldiers' personal entanglements right up to the morning the Japanese attack transforms their lives and the war overnight.

wins.title: On the Waterfront
wins.plotSummary: Terry Malloy, a washed-up boxer turned longshoreman, unwittingly helps set up a murder for the corrupt union boss who controls the docks, only to be wracked with guilt after falling for the victim's sister, Edie. Pressured by a crusading waterfront priest to testify against the mob-connected union leadership, Terry must weigh his own survival and loyalty to his brother against doing the right thing. Elia Kazan's drama builds to Terry's defiant testimony and a brutal confrontation on the docks that forces the other dockworkers to choose a side.

wins.title: Marty
wins.plotSummary: Marty Piletti, a good-natured but lonely 34-year-old Bronx butcher, is constantly pressured by his mother and friends to find a wife, having resigned himself to bachelorhood after years of romantic disappointment. At a dance hall one Saturday night, he meets Clara, a plain, socially awkward schoolteacher freshly abandoned by her blind date, and the two spend an unexpectedly tender evening together. Delbert Mann's intimate drama follows Marty's struggle to defy his friends' mockery and his own self-doubt to pursue the one connection that finally feels genuine.

wins.title: Around the World in 80 Days
wins.plotSummary: Confident Victorian gentleman Phileas Fogg wagers his entire fortune with fellow club members that he can circumnavigate the globe in just eighty days, setting off immediately with his resourceful French valet, Passepartout. Their journey by train, ship, elephant, and balloon carries them through a globe-trotting series of comic misadventures, romantic detours, and near-disasters, while a detective trails Fogg on suspicion of bank robbery. Michael Todd's star-studded epic turns Jules Verne's adventure into a sprawling travelogue racing against the clock.

wins.title: The Bridge on the River Kwai
wins.plotSummary: British POWs under the rigid, honor-obsessed Colonel Nicholson are forced to build a railway bridge for their Japanese captors in occupied Burma, with Nicholson gradually treating the bridge's construction as a matter of British pride rather than collaboration with the enemy. Meanwhile, an escaped American prisoner is recruited by British commandos to return and destroy the very bridge Nicholson has poured his soul into completing. David Lean's epic builds to a devastating climax in which Nicholson's obsession with his own achievement collides fatally with the mission to blow it up.

wins.title: Gigi
wins.plotSummary: In Belle Époque Paris, teenage Gigi is being groomed by her grandmother and eccentric great-aunt in the family tradition of becoming a wealthy man's mistress, all while enjoying an easy, unromantic friendship with the bored young bon vivant Gaston. As Gigi blossoms into womanhood, Gaston begins to see her differently, forcing both of them to reconsider whether their comfortable friendship could become something more respectable and lasting. Vincente Minnelli's musical, adapted from Colette's novella, turns a story about courtesan training into a wistful romance about growing up and changing expectations.

wins.title: Ben-Hur
wins.plotSummary: Judah Ben-Hur, a wealthy Jewish prince in Roman-occupied Jerusalem, is betrayed by his childhood friend Messala, now a Roman officer, and condemned to slavery while his mother and sister are imprisoned. After years as a galley slave, Ben-Hur earns his freedom and returns to Judea seeking revenge against Messala, culminating in a legendary chariot race between the two former friends. William Wyler's religious epic intertwines Ben-Hur's quest for vengeance with a parallel story of Christ's ministry and crucifixion, ultimately steering Ben-Hur from vengeance toward forgiveness.

wins.title: The Apartment
wins.plotSummary: C.C. "Bud" Baxter, a lonely insurance clerk, lends his Manhattan apartment to four philandering company managers in exchange for career favors, juggling their overlapping schedules while his own life stays empty. He falls for elevator operator Fran Kubelik, not realizing she's the mistress of Baxter's boss, the married personnel director Sheldrake, who also uses Baxter's apartment for their affair. Billy Wilder's bittersweet comedy follows Baxter's growing conscience as he chooses Fran's happiness over his own corporate advancement.

wins.title: West Side Story
wins.plotSummary: In 1957 New York, two rival teenage gangs, the white Jets and the Puerto Rican Sharks, feud for control of their Upper West Side turf, escalating toward a planned rumble. Tony, a Jets co-founder trying to leave gang life behind, falls instantly in love with Maria, the sister of Sharks leader Bernardo, at a neighborhood dance, igniting a forbidden romance that mirrors Romeo and Juliet. As tensions boil over into the rumble, the lovers' hopes for a life together collide fatally with the gangs' spiraling violence.

wins.title: Lawrence of Arabia
wins.plotSummary: T.E. Lawrence, an eccentric British Army lieutenant, is dispatched to assess the Arab tribal revolt against the Ottoman Empire during World War I and becomes an unlikely, charismatic leader of the uprising himself. Leading a grueling desert crossing and daring raids on Aqaba and Damascus, Lawrence forges a fierce bond with the Arab cause even as the violence and his own growing ego and cruelty change him in ways he can't control. David Lean's epic follows Lawrence's rise from obscure officer to legend, and his fracturing identity torn between British loyalty and his adopted Arab allegiance.

wins.title: Tom Jones
wins.plotSummary: Squire Allworthy discovers an abandoned infant in his bed and raises the boy, Tom Jones, as his own, and Tom grows into a good-hearted but impulsive young man beloved by women and devoted to his true love, Sophie Western. Sophie's family pushes her toward a more "suitable" match with the scheming Blifil, Allworthy's nephew, who manipulates circumstances to get Tom banished from the estate and out of Sophie's life. Tony Richardson's bawdy period comedy follows Tom's picaresque misadventures across the English countryside as he tries to clear his name and reclaim both his inheritance and Sophie's hand.

wins.title: My Fair Lady
wins.plotSummary: Phonetics professor Henry Higgins wagers that he can transform Cockney flower-seller Eliza Doolittle into a woman who can pass as a duchess simply by teaching her to speak proper English, taking her on as a live-in student and social experiment. As Eliza masters her diction and grace and triumphs at a high-society ball, she also begins to demand the respect and independence Higgins never intended to give her. The musical follows Eliza's transformation curdling into a battle over who she truly belongs to, herself or her creator.

wins.title: The Sound of Music
wins.plotSummary: Maria, a free-spirited postulant unsuited to convent life, is sent to serve as governess to the seven children of widowed naval Captain Georg von Trapp in Salzburg, winning them over with music after their father's strict, joyless household. Maria and the Captain fall in love and marry, but their happiness is threatened as Austria is annexed by Nazi Germany and the Captain is ordered to serve in the German navy. Robert Wise's musical follows the von Trapp family's dramatic escape over the mountains into Switzerland rather than submit to the Nazi regime.

wins.title: A Man for All Seasons
wins.plotSummary: Sir Thomas More, Lord Chancellor of England, refuses on principle to endorse King Henry VIII's annulment from Catherine of Aragon or swear the Oath of Supremacy declaring Henry head of the Church of England. As former allies and even his own family urge him toward a compromise that would save his career and his life, More holds to his conscience with quiet, unshakable resolve, even as the political cost mounts against him. Fred Zinnemann's drama follows More's principled stand to its inevitable, tragic conclusion at the hands of a king who will not tolerate dissent.

wins.title: In the Heat of the Night
wins.plotSummary: Virgil Tibbs, a Black Philadelphia police detective passing through Sparta, Mississippi, is wrongly arrested as a murder suspect before the local police realize he's a highly skilled homicide detective and reluctantly enlist his help solving the case. Tibbs and the town's bigoted police chief, Bill Gillespie, clash over race and authority even as they're forced to work together, their mutual respect growing grudgingly as the investigation unfolds. Norman Jewison's mystery-drama uses the murder case as a vehicle for confronting Deep South racism head-on through the tense partnership between the two men.

wins.title: Oliver!
wins.plotSummary: Orphaned boy Oliver Twist is sold into apprenticeship from a brutal workhouse and eventually runs away to London, where he falls in with a gang of child pickpockets led by the wily Fagin and his menacing associate, Bill Sikes. Oliver finds unexpected kindness from Nancy, Sikes's mistreated girlfriend, and from a wealthy gentleman who takes him in, only for Fagin's gang to drag him back into danger. Carol Reed's musical adaptation of Dickens follows Oliver's journey from institutional cruelty toward a rescue that costs Nancy her life.

wins.title: Midnight Cowboy
wins.plotSummary: Naive Texan Joe Buck moves to New York City dressed as a cowboy, hoping to make his fortune as a male prostitute for wealthy women, but finds the city colder and less profitable than he imagined. He falls in with Rico "Ratso" Rizzo, a sickly, scheming con man, and the two form an unlikely, increasingly tender friendship as they scrape by together in a condemned apartment. John Schlesinger's film follows their bond toward a bittersweet journey to Florida that Ratso's declining health may not let him survive.

wins.title: Patton
wins.plotSummary: During World War II, hard-charging General George S. Patton is sent to salvage the U.S. II Corps after its defeat at Kasserine Pass, whipping the demoralized unit into shape and leading it to victory at El Guettar. His rivalry with British commander Bernard Montgomery drives him through the Sicily campaign and across Europe, where his brilliance as a battlefield tactician is repeatedly undercut by his own vanity, temper, and political missteps, including a notorious incident of striking a shell-shocked soldier. The film follows Patton's unstoppable march through the war even as his outsized ego costs him command opportunities and public standing.

wins.title: The French Connection
wins.plotSummary: In New York, hard-nosed narcotics detectives Jimmy "Popeye" Doyle and Buddy Russo stumble onto a massive heroin-smuggling operation run by suave French trafficker Alain Charnier, who is trying to move $32 million worth of pure heroin into the country hidden in a television personality's car. As Doyle becomes fixated on catching Charnier, obsession curdles into a citywide cat-and-mouse chase that includes an explosive, high-speed car chase beneath an elevated subway line. William Friedkin's gritty procedural follows the investigation to its ambiguous, unresolved conclusion, with Charnier's fate left hanging.

wins.title: The Godfather
wins.plotSummary: As the aging patriarch Vito Corleone survives an assassination attempt, his reluctant youngest son Michael, a decorated war veteran who wanted no part of the family business, is drawn into the Corleone crime family's brutal world of loyalty and vengeance. Michael's calculated rise from outsider to ruthless Mafia boss unfolds against a backdrop of gang warfare among New York's crime families, marked by betrayals, assassinations, and a baptism intercut with a wave of coordinated killings. By the film's end, Michael has consolidated power completely, becoming the very thing he once swore he wouldn't.

wins.title: The Sting
wins.plotSummary: In 1936, grifter Johnny Hooker teams up with veteran con man Henry Gondorff after a job leaves his partner dead at the hands of ruthless crime boss Doyle Lonnegan. Seeking revenge, they assemble a crew of fellow grifters to run an elaborate "big con" on Lonnegan, building a fake off-track betting parlor to fleece him out of a fortune. George Roy Hill's Depression-era caper builds through misdirection after misdirection toward a final double-cross that outwits both Lonnegan and the audience.

wins.title: The Godfather Part II
wins.plotSummary: The film interweaves two parallel stories: in 1958, Michael Corleone, now the Corleone family's Don, ruthlessly consolidates power and defends against an assassination attempt, growing more isolated and paranoid even as his empire expands into Cuba and Nevada; in flashback, young Vito Corleone flees Sicily as a boy after his family is murdered and builds his own criminal empire from nothing in early-1900s New York. Vito's rise is shown as one of loyalty and family, while Michael's parallel ascent becomes one of betrayal and loneliness, culminating in the destruction of his own family bonds.

wins.title: One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest
wins.plotSummary: Randle McMurphy, a con man who fakes insanity to serve his sentence in a mental institution rather than a prison work farm, clashes immediately with the ward's controlling, punitive head nurse, Ratched. McMurphy's rebellious energy inspires the other patients to reclaim small freedoms, from a covert fishing trip to a late-night party, but his defiance draws escalating retaliation from the hospital's authority. The confrontation between McMurphy and Ratched ends in tragedy, with McMurphy lobotomized into a vegetative state and a fellow patient, Chief Bromden, finally escaping the institution in his place.
